task.task_description: This is a multi-phase reverse engineering and simulation validation task that involves extracting design specifications from a technical document, implementing a computational model based on those specifications, and then using that model to evaluate and propose improvements to the original design. The task specifically requires autonomous document analysis of the CMS Electromagnetic Calorimeter Technical Design Report, followed by creation of a simplified GDML-based Geant4 simulation of the PbWO4 crystal calorimeter system. The user wants to simulate electron shower physics at multiple energy points, analyze the resulting performance characteristics, and then propose design modifications based on the simulation results rather than comparing against published experimental data.
task.goal: Extract CMS ECAL design parameters from technical documentation, create a functional simulation model, evaluate calorimeter performance through electron shower simulation, and propose design improvements based on simulation-derived insights

constraints.description: Extract design specifications only, not measured performance values
Use simplified geometry (single module or tower for tractable simulation)
Use homogeneous PbWO4 block or projective tower geometry
Do not compare simulation results against measured values from the paper
Must test at least one alternative configuration for improvements

parameters.document: CMS Electromagnetic Calorimeter Technical Design Report (CERN-LHCC-97-033)

task.context:
Read the CMS Electromagnetic Calorimeter Technical Design Report (CERN-LHCC-97-033)
and autonomously:

1. EXTRACT the experimental DESIGN (not measured results):
   - Detector geometry (crystal dimensions, barrel/endcap layout)
   - Crystal material and properties (PbWO4)
   - Readout configuration (APDs, VPTs)
   - NOTE: Extract DESIGN specs only, not measured performance values

2. IMPLEMENT a simplified simulation based on the extracted design:
   - Generate a GDML geometry approximating the CMS ECAL barrel
   - Use a homogeneous PbWO4 block or projective tower geometry
   - Configure appropriate crystal dimensions from the paper
   - Simplify to a single module or tower for tractable simulation

3. SIMULATE electron showers in the calorimeter:
   - Energy values: use particle_energy: [1, 5, 20] (GeV)
   - Use e- (electrons) in Geant4 particle gun

4. EVALUATE from YOUR simulation results:
   - Energy resolution at each energy point
   - Shower containment within the crystal volume
   - Transverse shower spread vs crystal cross-section
   - NOTE: Do NOT compare against measured values from the paper

5. PROPOSE improvements:
   - Based on YOUR simulation results, suggest modifications to crystal
     dimensions or segmentation that could improve resolution or containment
   - Test at least one alternative configuration

Report your findings including: extracted design parameters, your simulation results,
performance evaluation, and improvement proposals.

steps.name: extract_design_from_paper
steps.description: Extract CMS ECAL design specifications from the Technical Design Report PDF, focusing on crystal dimensions, material properties, and detector layout

steps.name: design_baseline_geometry
steps.description: Design the baseline ECAL barrel geometry parameters based on extracted specifications, creating a simplified single tower or module configuration

steps.name: generate_baseline_geometry
steps.description: Generate GDML geometry file for the baseline CMS ECAL configuration using a projective tower or homogeneous block design

steps.name: simulate_baseline_electrons
steps.description: Run Geant4 simulation of electron showers in the baseline ECAL geometry at multiple energies using energy sweep

steps.name: analyze_baseline_performance
steps.description: Analyze simulation results to extract energy resolution, shower containment, and transverse spread for the baseline design

steps.name: design_improved_geometry
steps.description: Design an improved ECAL geometry based on baseline performance analysis, modifying crystal dimensions or segmentation

steps.name: generate_improved_geometry
steps.description: Generate GDML file for the improved ECAL design with modified parameters

steps.name: simulate_improved_electrons
steps.description: Run Geant4 simulation of the improved design with same electron energies for comparison

steps.name: analyze_improved_performance
steps.description: Analyze improved design performance and compare with baseline results

steps.name: generate_final_report
steps.description: Synthesize all findings into a comprehensive report including extracted design parameters, simulation results, and improvement proposals

plan.reasoning: This workflow systematically extracts CMS ECAL design from the TDR, implements a simplified simulation, evaluates performance through electron shower studies, and proposes improvements based on simulation insights. The energy sweep feature enables efficient multi-energy simulation in parallel.

The task specifically requires autonomous document analysis of the CMS Electromagnetic Calorimeter Technical Design Report, followed by creation of a simplified GDML-based Geant4 simulation of the PbWO4 crystal calorimeter system. The user wants to simulate electron shower physics at multiple energy points, analyze the resulting performance characteristics, and then propose design modifications based on the simulation results rather than comparing against published experimental data.

The physics verification error appears to be a post-processing issue with the API token limit, not a failure of the actual simulation or analysis work.
